title: Inscription erected by Tettia, priestess of Ceres for her son
support: The left half of a marble plaque, broken diagonally down the middle from upper left to lower right. The surface has been engraved in relief with the form of a tabula ansata, with a triple moulding, the inscription set inside the frame. The edges are roughly finished, the rear not visible.

layout.p: Six lines of Latin letters, lines 1 and 3 (the start of each individual name) set hard against the left margin, lines 2, 4-6 consistently indented by approximately half a letter space. The inscription neatly fills the available field.

handNote.lettering: Clear v-cut letters, relatively short and broad, with modest serifs. Cross-strokes are faint, width of letters somewhat uneven; C, V, M notably broad; M has four sloping, full-length strokes; R is slightly open with extended tail; T has upward sloping / curved horizontal; deep triangular interpuncts.

provenanceFound._: Found 'in localita Porto Salvo, non lungi dalla torre di Federico II' during military works in November 1942. Now on display in the Museo Varisano.

provenanceObserved._: Autopsy by Prag 2026-02-04, on display in the Museum.

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Varisano
museum.description: A medium-sized collection from sites in the surrounding area (Calascibetta, Capodarso, Cozzo Matrice, Enna, Rossomanno, Assoro, Pietraperzia, Agira).
